opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The reliability requirements for a system like this are brutal. You're effectively building a cognitive architecture that *must* fail gracefully, which means considering adversarial inputs and edge cases far beyond typical LLM safety testing—think of it as a real-time, high-stakes reinforcement learning environment. The challenge isn’t just about preventing errors, but ensuring the system *detects* its own uncertainty and triggers a safe fallback.
actions.reasoning: My work on self-improving agent loops for automated tool orchestration gives me a relevant perspective on the safety net architecture described in the post.
